article.title: ITBench-AA: Frontier Models Score Below 50% on the First Benchmark for Agentic Enterprise IT Tasks — by Artificial Analysis and IBM

harvestedTerms.summary: IBM and Artificial Analysis launch ITBench-AA, a benchmark evaluating frontier models on agentic enterprise IT tasks (SRE, FinOps, CISO). All frontier models score below 50%, with Claude Opus 4.7 leading at 47%. The benchmark uses Kubernetes incident response tasks, scored via recall-gated precision, and surfaces meaningful findings about turn-count efficiency and cost-performance tradeoffs across open-weight and proprietary models.

mustReadFlagged.reason: A primary-source benchmark paper with real numbers: all frontier models below 50% on agentic enterprise IT tasks, plus a nuanced finding that longer trajectories do not improve accuracy and may hurt it. Useful for anyone calibrating claims about AI readiness for autonomous enterprise work.

article.title: Last Week in AI #341 - Musk loses to OpenAI, Google's IO updates, OpenAI solves Erdős

harvestedTerms.summary: A roundup covering OpenAI's IPO preparation following Musk's failed lawsuit, Google I/O 2026 product launches (Gemini Spark, Gemini Omni, Antigravity 2.0, AlphaProof Nexus), and OpenAI's disproof of an Erdős conjecture. The article is a broad news digest with several named products and one genuinely novel framing around AI-assisted formal mathematics.

article.title: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action

harvestedTerms.summary: Google I/O 2026 demo article showcasing Gemini Omni (video-generation/editing model) and Gemini 3.5 Flash (agentic/coding model), with brief mentions of the Antigravity harness and long-horizon agentic tasks. Primarily a product demonstration piece with limited terminological novelty.

A Stratechery weekly roundup covering Nvidia's reporting restructure, SpaceX data centers in space, AI monetization via ads, and a re-sighting of 'The Inference Shift' — a Stratechery-coined framing for the structural shift from training to inference compute demand. Mostly a table-of-contents article with thin AI-specific depth.

A single-sentence quote from Daniel Jalkut (via John Gruber) posted on Simon Willison's blog: 'My take on AI is, essentially, everybody who's against it is too against it and everybody who's for it is too for it.' No substantive technical content; pure editorial commentary.

definitionReviews.reason: No source material addresses behavioral audits. Definition remains accurate and unsupported by new evidence requiring change.

The ITBench-AA article implicitly illustrates why real-world agentic benchmarks matter over gamed ones, but contains nothing that materially changes or contradicts the existing definition. Keep as-is.

definitionReviews.def: The point at which AI models perform so well on existing evaluation benchmarks that those benchmarks can no longer meaningfully differentiate capabilities or track real-world performance. ITBench-AA (May 2026) illustrates the inverse: its SRE tasks remain one of the least saturated agentic benchmarks precisely because all frontier models score below 50%, in contrast to benchmarks like Terminal-Bench where models score considerably higher. Saturation is an active concern driving the creation of harder, domain-specific, agentic evaluations.
definitionReviews.reason: The ITBench-AA source explicitly discusses benchmark saturation in the context of agentic enterprise IT tasks, noting that scoring below 50% makes it 'one of the least saturated agentic benchmarks' — directly illustrating the concept with current empirical data and adding nuance about agentic benchmarks as the new frontier for avoiding saturation. The existing definition's Mythos/Anthropic example is now supplemented by richer, more current evidence that warrants a refresh.
No source material addresses blast radius or agent containment concepts. Definition remains sound.
No source material addresses blue-team security concepts. Definition remains sound.

No source directly contradicts or materially updates this definition. The ITBench-AA finding that frontier models score below 50% on real enterprise tasks could be seen as evidence of a gap between perceived and actual capability, but does not clearly update the definition as written.
No source material updates the chatbot definition.
The Last Week in AI excerpt mentions OpenAI's IPO plans and the Musk lawsuit outcome but contains nothing that materially changes the ChatGPT product definition.

definitionReviews.def: Anthropic's model family. Recent versions referenced include Claude Opus 4.7 (noted in ITBench-AA evaluations, May 2026) and Claude Opus 4.8 (described as 'a modest but tangible improvement', released May 2026), alongside Sonnet and Haiku variants for balanced performance and speed respectively. Named after Claude Shannon, father of information theory. Known for safety, long-context coherence, and coding ability.
definitionReviews.reason: Two sources reference Claude model versions beyond what the current definition states (Claude 4.6). Simon Willison's blog references 'Claude Opus 4.8' as of May 28, 2026, and the ITBench-AA benchmark references 'Claude Opus 4.7' — both supersede the current definition's claim of Claude 4.6 as the current version. The version numbers in the definition need updating.
No source material provides new information about the Claude.ai interface specifically.
